subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Why You Should Not Induce Vomiting
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Why You Should Not Induce Vomiting.

hook.text: For decades, parents kept ipecac syrup to make kids vomit after poisoning. It caused more harm.

buildup.text: The logic seemed sound: get the poison out the way it went in.

discovery.text: Vomiting caustic substances causes the chemical to burn the esophagus a second time.

twist.text: Petroleum products can be aspirated into the lungs during vomiting, causing chemical pneumonia.

climax.text: Medical guidelines now recommend against inducing vomiting for almost all poisonings.

punchline.text: Don't make them vomit. Call poison control. Let the experts decide.

quiz.question: Why is inducing vomiting dangerous after swallowing a caustic substance?
options: It burns the esophagus a second time coming back up
It dilutes the poison and spreads it
It causes the stomach to rupture
